mainFeedback.title: Samira: stop giving wins back
mainFeedback.focus: Honest read: your biggest leak is not damage. It is refusing to cash out: staying at lethal HP, re-fighting after winning, and chasing sideways when structure is already open.
mainFeedback.rule: If the next action does not create HP safety, wave crash, turret, or nexus pressure, do not take it.
mainFeedback.nextRep: Next game: cash out before the second fight.
mainFeedback.whyTrust: The clips show the same leak in three forms: lethal-HP lane stay, low-health re-fight, and open-base drift. That is specific enough to practice immediately.
mainFeedback.pattern: You already find fights. The coachable problem is making the second decision boring enough to actually win: reset, crash, hit tower, hit nexus, then fight again only if it protects that payout.
checklist: First win becomes wave or structure.
Open base means no sideways chase.
mainFeedback.reviewLimit: Replay review is based on sampled frames and visible state, not raw inputs or full cooldown telemetry.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstay After Successful Fight—No Immediate Reset or Objective
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After winning a fight and surviving with low HP, you stayed on the map instead of recalling or converting for structure or dragon. Fix: Immediately recall or take the nearest objective after a successful fight instead of overextending for extra minions or a second fight.
recordings.gameDetail: A classic bot lane snowball start: you barely escape early aggression, survive at lethal HP after trades, and then score a kill despite being pushed out. But post-fight, with both waves and HP in a fragile state, you lingered in lane or just outside tower instead of using the recall window or crashing for a clear objective. This gives up your advantage window and risks your shutdown to respawned opponents or jungle pressure. Around 8:35, Samira is still in lane and fighting after an earlier win instead of recalling or taking a safe objective, showing the overstay with risky HP; By 14:01, Samira, allied, and enemy champions near mid inhibitor; Maokai and Morgana visible; setup for the reset decision, where the next check is recall, spend, or keep risking the shutdown. If you win a fight or escape on low HP, cash out immediately with a recall or map-changing action—don't wait for the enemy to respawn or regroup.
recordings.eventEvidence: 8:35 Samira is still in lane and fighting after an earlier win instead of recalling or taking a safe objective, showing the overstay with risky HP.; 14:01 Samira, allied, and enemy champions near mid inhibitor; Maokai and Morgana visible; setup for the reset decision, where the next check is recall, spend, or keep risking the shutdown.
recordings.goodThing: You capitalized on an enemy overextension and found a clean 1v1 outplay when the opportunity showed up early.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira's kit spikes after cashing out and buying, and if you die with shutdown, the enemy gets right back in the game; this pattern is visible every game at this rank.
recordings.evidence: 8:35 Samira is still in lane and fighting after an earlier win instead of recalling or taking a safe objective, showing the overstay with risky HP.; 14:01 Samira, allied, and enemy champions near mid inhibitor; Maokai and Morgana visible; setup for the reset decision, where the next check is recall, spend, or keep risking the shutdown.
recordings.pattern: After surviving high-threat situations or securing a key kill, you linger to pick up extra farm or fight in extended skirmishes when a clean recall or conversion is open. This habit exposes you to enemy reset or jungle threat, especially holding a shutdown.
recordings.diamondRule: After every fight you win or survive at low HP, reset immediately or convert to nearby objectives—do not give the enemy an easy comeback.
recordings.drill: In your next queue, after your first fight or skirmish win, hard commit to recall or directly take an objective on your next wave, no matter how tempting extra minions or pokes are.
